heading: International geothermal energy development
label: 17203 International geothermal energy development
notes: Amendments2020—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary of Energy, in coordination with other appropriate Federal and multilateral agencies (including the United States Agency for International Development) shall support international collaborative efforts to promote the research, development, and deployment of geothermal technologies used to develop hydrothermal and enhanced geothermal system resources, including as partners (as appropriate) the African Rift Geothermal Development Facility, Australia, China, France, the Republic of Iceland, India, Japan, and the United Kingdom.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(k)(1)Subsec. (c). , struck out subsec. (c). Text read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(k)(2)
Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2
amendments: Amendments2020—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary of Energy, in coordination with other appropriate Federal and multilateral agencies (including the United States Agency for International Development) shall support international collaborative efforts to promote the research, development, and deployment of geothermal technologies used to develop hydrothermal and enhanced geothermal system resources, including as partners (as appropriate) the African Rift Geothermal Development Facility, Australia, China, France, the Republic of Iceland, India, Japan, and the United Kingdom.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(k)(1)Subsec. (c). , struck out subsec. (c). Text read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.”Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(k)(2)

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Energy, in coordination with other appropriate Federal and multilateral agencies (including the United States Agency for International Development) shall support collaborative efforts with international partners to promote the research, development, and demonstration of geothermal technologies used to develop hydrothermal and enhanced geothermal system resources.

children.heading: United States Trade and Development Agency
children.label: b United States Trade and Development Agency

children.text: encourage participation by United States firms in actions taken to carry out subsection (a); and
provide grants and other financial support for feasibility and resource assessment studies conducted in, or intended to benefit, less developed countries.
